content.text:
# Design: cross-invocation lookup cache

## Approach

Persist lookup results to a single JSON file under the user's home directory (mirroring the existing ~/.toolrc precedent from src/config.ts:8). On each call to fetchRemote(key) in src/lookup.ts:42, read the cache file, return the entry when its timestamp is within the one-hour staleness window from docs/api.md:30, otherwise fetch and write back.

## Tradeoffs and alternatives

Alternative A — in-memory cache: rejected because each CLI invocation is a fresh process (src/cli.ts:11), so memory does not survive between runs. Alternative B — external cache service: rejected by the self-contained constraint. The file-based approach trades a small amount of disk I/O for correctness across processes, which is the right call given sub-2KB blobs.

## Risks

Concurrent invocations could race on the cache file; mitigate with an atomic write-and-rename. Clock skew could serve stale entries; bound the window conservatively.
